channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: Nightdive re-announce SiN Reloaded with the promise that it's coming out this year
item.content:encoded:
Another one in the chamber

Video games are weird little things. It's pretty rare for films to get scrapped part way through production, even if these days some production companies opt to use even finished projects as tax write offs. But games can frequently get canned, disappear for a bit, and even return after many years of silence. Enter SiN Reloaded, the Nightdive-made remaster of the classic boomer shooter, that is alive once more!

Back in 2023, Nightdive essentially parked SiN Reloaded due to other projects they were tackling at the time, promising that the remaster wasn't dead, and that it was just going through some changes. Well, it seems like they've now given the game the birds and the bees talk, as they've now, uh, announced it once more, which isn't really how that works given they already did that once, but hey ho, PR is going to PR. Not only has it been announced, but it has a release year: this one! The year of 2026, by my count.

It's also looking pretty swish! I've not played the original SiN myself, but a quick comparison suggests the spirit is certainly there. I'm sure someone could find something to complain about, this world is much too large for any of us to get off scott free there, but the butchering of original material does not appear to be taking place. Plus, you can also opt to play using the original SiN Gold version's graphics! Even less to complain about.

For those who've never played SiN, it's one of those near-future dystopias where you play a totally cool, badass, macho guy who goes by Blade (not the vampire one) taking on an evil doctor called Elexis Sinclaire (Elexis really seems like a typo) who's made a DNA-altering drug. "As Blade, you've made a religion out of fighting crime. Now you're going to make Elexis pay for her sins by taking on her unholy army of genetically-engineered mutants," explains the game's Steam page.

There's no exact release date yet, just that 2026 window, but you can always wishlist it on Steam in the meantime.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/nightdive-re-announce-sin-reloaded-with-the-promise-that-its-coming-out-this-year
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: As of today, your RuneScape subscription will be just that little bit more expensive every month
item.content:encoded:
More buck, same amount of bang

To pay for an MMO subscription is to unfortunately know that you will eventually have to pay more than you'd like to because prices never go down, now do they. Today, the MMO in question is RuneScape, with Jagex announcing an increase for both monthly and yearly subscriptions, the second price increase in under two years.

Before, you'd be paying $14 a month if you were paying month to month, but that's now going up by a whole dollar to $15. If you're someone that opts for a 12 month membership, that's now gone up from $99.48 to $131.88 a year, a not insignificant jump when you add it all up, even if you are technically still saving money compared to a monthly membership.

Alongside the price increase it's also been announced that you'll no longer be able to get a six month membership package, unless you already have one. In that case, it will keep rolling on until you either change it or cancel it. On top of that, anyone that's on a monthly recurring grandfathered rate will be able to continue paying for that price. For context, this applies to anyone that's been paying for a subscription since before September 27th, 2024, so those of you newer to the game will have to deal with the increased cost.

Jagex explain the hike in an FAQ by putting it down to three points: that it "better supports the ongoing investment we are making across both games," it, "supports the cost of infrastructure, upgraded systems, new content, and improvements to player support," and it, "keeps RuneScape and Old School RuneScape sustainable for the next 25 years, not just the last 25."

The FAQ also makes a note of how "most major MMOs rely on expansions, battle passes, or increasingly aggressive monetisation to account for increased investment and cost of operation. We believe in a simple subscription-led approach that delivers incredible value while supporting continued improvement for our games and services."

Not an ideal situation for many I'm sure, so let's just hope this is the last increase for a while.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/as-of-today-your-runescape-subscription-will-be-just-that-little-bit-more-expensive-every-month
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: It's not a mirage, Crimson Desert's system requirements are here, and don't seem half bad
item.content:encoded:
The RPG launches next week

The everything but the kitchen sink RPG Crimson Desert is finally out in a little over a week, but those of us in the realm of personal computers have been left wondering what it is exactly we'll need to run the thing (I'm sure those flashy particle effects take their toll). Well, wonder no longer, intrepid Crimson Deserter, Pearly Abyss have released the PC specs you'll need to be sporting to get this thing running.

No matter what settings you play on, there's three things you'll need for sure: 16GB of RAM, Windows 10 or newer, and 150GB of SSD storage space free. That last one won't be particularly ideal for many folks out there, but such is the cost of playing a big fancy AAA title on your computer in this day and age. The minimum specs are a tad more forgiving, requiring an AMD Radeon RX 5500 XT or Nvidia GeForce GTX 1060 for your GPU, and an AMD Ryzen 5 2600X or Intel i5-8500 CPU to play at an upscaled 1080p from 900p at 30 FPS.

That of course does jump way up when you hit the other end at the ultra settings, allowing for 4K and 60FPS, but for a GPU you'll need an AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT or an Nvidia GeForce RTX 5070 Ti, and for a CPU you'll need an AMD Ryzen 7 7700X or an Intel i5-13600K. There is also a caveat from Pearl Abyss that notes "figures shown are based on internal testing results. Performance may vary depending on your PC’s hardware and software configuration, as well as changes made to individual graphics settings." As well as this, you will need the latest DirectX 12.

We still don't know how good the thing is or isn't yet, so, hard to say whether those of you can't play it for spec reasons are missing out on anything. We'll find out next week! As it launches on March 19th. At least if you are picking it up, you won't have to worry about microtransactions.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/its-not-a-mirage-crimson-deserts-system-requirements-are-here-and-dont-seem-half-bad
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
